records.ety: [L. postponere, postpositum; post after + ponere to place, put. See Post-, and Position.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Postponed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Postponing.]

defs: To defer to a future or later time; to put off; also, to cause to be deferred or put off; to delay; to adjourn; as, to postpone the consideration of a bill to the following day, or indefinitely.
quotes: His praise postponed, and never to be paid.

defs: To place after, behind, or below something, in respect to precedence, preference, value, or importance.
quotes: All other considerations should give way and be postponed to this.
